$schema_note: Flat map of repo-relative file path -> {description, depends_on, dependents}. depends_on/dependents are populated for .py and .ts/.tsx files only (computed from real imports); other files have empty lists. Generated for LLM-agent orientation; not wired into CLAUDE.md.
root: rayflow — visual workflow orchestration on Ray

.claude/hooks/_file_map.py.description: Shared helper module (not a hook itself) used by every hook script here: loads rayflow_file_map.json, resolves an absolute file_path to a repo-relative path, and extracts live function/class signatures from a .py file via ast (never cached, so it can't go stale like the JSON descriptions can).

.claude/hooks/dependents_reminder.py.description: PostToolUse hook (Edit|Write): after editing a mapped file, reminds Claude which other files list it as a dependent, so knock-on effects (like an outdated test) aren't forgotten.

.claude/hooks/file_map_context.py.description: PreToolUse hook (Read|Edit|Write): before Claude touches a mapped file, surfaces its description plus depends_on/dependents from rayflow_file_map.json, and for .py files also lists real function/class signatures extracted live via ast — always accurate regardless of whether the JSON description itself is stale.

.claude/hooks/prompt_file_hints.py.description: UserPromptSubmit hook: if the user's prompt names something matching a mapped file's basename (e.g. "validator", "NodeCard"), surfaces that file's description as additionalContext.

.claude/hooks/session_orientation.py.description: SessionStart hook: injects a one-line pointer to rayflow_file_map.json's existence (file count, root description) so a fresh session knows to consult it.

.claude/hooks/staleness_guard.py.description: PostToolUse hook (Write|Bash): heuristically flags when rayflow_file_map.json may be out of date — a new file was written that isn't in the map, or a shell command looks like it deletes a file that is.

.claude/settings.json.description: Project-level Claude Code settings (shared via git) — permissions and harness configuration for this repo.

.claude/settings.local.json.description: Machine-local Claude Code settings override, not meant to be shared/committed conventions aside.

.github/workflows/cla.yml.description: CLA Assistant workflow: asks external contributors to sign the CLA before a PR can merge; skips the check entirely for PRs opened by the maintainer.

.github/workflows/pypi.yaml.description: CI workflow that builds and publishes the package to PyPI on release.

.github/workflows/test.yml.description: CI workflow that runs the pytest suite on pushes/PRs.

.gitignore.description: Patterns for files/directories git should not track (build artifacts, virtualenvs, caches, etc.).

.mcp.json.description: Registers Rayflow's own MCP server (served at /mcp by a running `rayflow serve`) so Claude Code can use its tools directly.

CLA.md.description: Contributor License Agreement: contributors keep copyright but grant the maintainer the right to relicense their contributions, including commercially.

CLAUDE.md.description: Architecture and conventions guide for Claude Code (or any LLM agent) working in this repo: node system, engine internals, REST/MCP API tables, flow JSON schema.

COMMERCIAL-LICENSE.md.description: Explains Rayflow's dual-license model (AGPL-3.0-or-later free for non-commercial use; a separate commercial license is required otherwise) and how to obtain one.

CONTRIBUTING.md.description: Contributor guide: dev setup, how the CLA bot signing flow works, PR guidelines.

LICENSE.description: Full text of the GNU Affero General Public License v3.0, Rayflow's open-source license.

MANIFEST.in.description: setuptools sdist manifest: excludes CLAUDE.md, docs/, and examples/ from the published PyPI package.

README.md.description: Project overview, installation, quickstart, and license summary shown on GitHub and PyPI.

docs/analisis-conceptual.md.description: Long-form conceptual analysis (in Spanish) of Rayflow's execution model: the two superimposed planes (control/exec and data) and the core mental model behind the engine.

docs/frontend.md.description: Quick-reference (in Spanish) inventory of the editor frontend's tech stack and component responsibilities.

docs/propuesta-runcontext.md.description: Design proposal (in Spanish) that motivated reifying a flow execution as a `RunContext` object to isolate concurrent runs.

docs/rayflow.md.description: Historical v1 design document (in Spanish, explicitly marked obsolete) describing the original workflow-orchestration model, kept as an intent record.

examples/branch_demo.json.description: Example flow demonstrating conditional branching with the Branch node.

examples/cast_demo.json.description: Example flow demonstrating explicit type casting nodes (ToInt/ToFloat/ToStr/ToBool).

examples/foreach_demo.json.description: Example flow demonstrating iteration over a list with the ForEach node.

examples/sequence_demo.json.description: Example flow demonstrating ordered sequential execution with the Sequence node.

examples/showcase.json.description: Larger example flow combining multiple node types to showcase Rayflow's model end-to-end.

examples/suma.json.description: Minimal example flow: adds two integers (OnStart -> Add -> FlowOutput).

pyproject.toml.description: Python package metadata: dependencies, build system, author/license info, pytest config, and package-data inclusion rules.

rayflow/__init__.py.description: Package entry point: re-exports the public Python API (run/load/unload/execute/serve_events/stop) and exposes rayflow.__version__ resolved from installed package metadata.

rayflow/__main__.py.description: Lets the package run as `python -m rayflow`, delegating to the CLI.

rayflow/api.py.description: High-level Python API: load/unload/execute/execute_async/reconnect_async/run/serve_events/stop — the functions used by the CLI, the REST routes, and the MCP tools to drive flows in and out of Ray.

rayflow/build/__init__.py.description: Re-exports build() as the package's public symbol.

rayflow/build/validator.py.description: Validates a parsed FlowDef against the node catalog and produces an executable BuiltFlow: flattens CallFlow subflows into one graph, checks types/wiring/cycles, and either raises on the first error (build()) or collects every error in one pass (validate_all()) for editor/MCP clients.

rayflow/cli/__init__.py.description: Re-exports the click CLI group as `main`.

rayflow/cli/main.py.description: Click-based CLI: defines `rayflow serve`, which initializes Ray and starts the REST/editor/MCP server.

rayflow/editor/__init__.py.description: Empty package marker for the editor subpackage.

rayflow/editor/custom_nodes_routes.py.description: FastAPI router for CRUD on custom node .py files (create/read/update/delete) plus hot-reloading the node catalog from disk without restarting the server.

rayflow/editor/examples/__init__.py.description: Marks examples/ as a Python package so its *.json files can be included as package-data and shipped with the pip install.

rayflow/editor/examples/branch_demo.json.description: Packaged copy of examples/branch_demo.json, served via GET /editor/examples and the MCP get_example tool.

rayflow/editor/examples/cast_demo.json.description: Packaged copy of examples/cast_demo.json, served via GET /editor/examples and the MCP get_example tool.

rayflow/editor/examples/foreach_demo.jso.description: Packaged copy of examples/foreach_demo.json, served via GET /editor/examples and the MCP get_example tool.

rayflow/editor/examples/sequence_demo.js.description: Packaged copy of examples/sequence_demo.json, served via GET /editor/examples and the MCP get_example tool.

rayflow/editor/examples/showcase.json.description: Packaged copy of examples/showcase.json, served via GET /editor/examples and the MCP get_example tool.

rayflow/editor/examples/suma.json.description: Packaged copy of examples/suma.json, served via GET /editor/examples and the MCP get_example tool.

rayflow/editor/frontend/.gitignore.description: Standard Git ignore for Node.js projects, excludes build artifacts, node_modules, and editor temp files.

rayflow/editor/frontend/README.md.description: Project documentation describing the React + TypeScript + Vite template setup and ESLint configuration guidance.

rayflow/editor/frontend/components.json.description: shadcn/ui configuration file specifying component paths, aliases, and Tailwind CSS settings.

rayflow/editor/frontend/eslint.config.js.description: ESLint configuration enforcing TypeScript, React best practices, and React hooks linting rules.

rayflow/editor/frontend/index.html.description: HTML entry point that bootstraps the React app with root div and main.tsx script.

rayflow/editor/frontend/package-lock.jso.description: Generated npm lockfile pinning exact frontend dependency versions; not hand-edited.

rayflow/editor/frontend/package.json.description: Dependency manifest with React, xyflow, shadcn/ui, CodeMirror, Tailwind CSS, and build/lint scripts.

rayflow/editor/frontend/public/favicon.s.description: Browser tab icon in SVG format for the Rayflow editor application.

rayflow/editor/frontend/public/icons.svg.description: SVG sprite sheet containing reusable icon definitions for the UI.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/App.css.description: Global and template styles for the main app layout and hero section (legacy/boilerplate).

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/App.tsx.description: Main application component orchestrating the flow editor UI with header, sidebars, canvas, properties panel, and runs panel.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/assets/hero..description: Promotional/hero image asset for the editor UI.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/assets/react.description: React logo asset used in template/demo content.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/assets/vite..description: Vite logo asset used in template/demo content.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/components/C.description: Full-screen code editor for editing Python custom node source with CodeMirror, syntax highlighting, and save functionality.
Collapsible sidebar panel for managing custom Python node files with create, edit, and delete operations.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/components/F.description: Visual flow graph editor using React Flow library, supporting node drag-drop, edge connections, live run visualization, and animation timing controls.
Modal dialog for editing flow input/output pins with type selection and duplicate name validation.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/components/N.description: React Flow node component displaying node metadata, exec/data pins with type colors, runtime badges, and run status indicators.
Categorized sidebar panel for dragging built-in and custom nodes onto the canvas with search filtering.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/components/P.description: Right sidebar showing selected node details, editable literal inputs, connected pins, validation errors, and output specs.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/components/R.description: Bottom panel for flow execution with input parameter entry, run history, and live output/error display during execution.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/components/V.description: Left sidebar section for managing flow variables (name, type, default value) with inline editing.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/components/u.description: shadcn/ui Accordion primitive: expandable/collapsible sections using Radix UI.
shadcn/ui Badge primitive: small colored label/chip component with variant support.
shadcn/ui Button primitive: clickable button with variant and size options (default, outline, ghost, destructive).
shadcn/ui Dialog primitive: modal popup with header, content, and footer sections from Radix UI.
shadcn/ui Input primitive: text input field component with native HTML integration.
shadcn/ui ScrollArea primitive: custom scrollbar styling wrapper using Radix UI.
shadcn/ui Select primitive: dropdown menu component with trigger, content, and option items.
shadcn/ui Separator primitive: horizontal or vertical divider line.
shadcn/ui Tabs primitive: tabbed interface with list and content panels from Radix UI.
shadcn/ui Textarea primitive: multi-line text input field.
shadcn/ui Tooltip primitive: hover-activated floating label from Radix UI.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/hooks/useRes.description: Custom hook for draggable sidebar/panel resizing with localStorage persistence and min/max constraints.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/hooks/useRun.description: Custom hook managing flow execution via SSE streaming, event batching for animation, and run reconnection on disconnect.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/index.css.description: Global styles defining dark theme color tokens, React Flow overrides, node card styling, and type color mapping.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/lib/api.ts.description: API client layer defining TypeScript types and fetch wrappers for backend endpoints (flows, nodes, runs, custom nodes).

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/lib/rayflowH.description: CodeMirror syntax highlighting and hover tooltips for Rayflow Python node decorators, pin declarations, and context methods.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/lib/translat.description: Bidirectional converters between React Flow graph format and Rayflow FlowDef JSON, with exec pin logic and type color mapping.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/lib/utils.ts.description: Utility functions exporting a className composition helper (cn) combining clsx and Tailwind merge.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/main.tsx.description: React app entry point that renders the root App component into the DOM.

rayflow/editor/frontend/src/store/flowSt.description: Zustand store managing editor state including flow tabs, node/edge graph, runs, validation errors, and localStorage persistence.

rayflow/editor/frontend/tsconfig.app.jso.description: TypeScript compiler config for the application source code with path aliases and strict type checking.

rayflow/editor/frontend/tsconfig.json.description: Root TypeScript config referencing app and node build configs.

rayflow/editor/frontend/tsconfig.node.js.description: TypeScript config for build tools and Vite configuration files.

rayflow/editor/frontend/vite.config.ts.description: Vite build configuration with React plugin, path aliases, API proxying, and build output to ../static/dist.

rayflow/editor/guide.py.description: Curated markdown guide to Rayflow's flow model, served by GET /editor/guide and the MCP get_guide tool as the semantic contract an LLM agent needs to build flows.

rayflow/editor/routes.py.description: FastAPI router for the visual editor: node catalog, type info, flow CRUD, validation, load/run/test execution endpoints, guide/examples, and event subscription — the same logic the MCP tools reuse.

rayflow/editor/static/dist/assets/index-.description: Generated build artifact (hashed bundle output of `npm run build`); not hand-edited.
Generated build artifact (hashed bundle output of `npm run build`); not hand-edited.

rayflow/editor/static/dist/favicon.svg.description: Generated build asset, copied from the frontend's public/ directory.

rayflow/editor/static/dist/icons.svg.description: Generated build asset, copied from the frontend's public/ directory.

rayflow/editor/static/dist/index.html.description: Generated production build output of the editor frontend (run `npm run build`); not meant to be edited by hand.

rayflow/editor/storage.py.description: Flow persistence: reads/writes flow JSON files under the workspace's flows/ directory and serializes a FlowDef back to a JSON-safe dict.

rayflow/engine/__init__.py.description: Re-exports the engine's public symbols: LoadedFlow and the load/unload/get/is-loaded helpers.

rayflow/engine/executor.py.description: The core execution engine: the FlowEngine Ray actor (runs a BuiltFlow, dispatching @ray_node/@engine_node/CallFlow nodes, resolving data pins, emitting SSE events) and LoadedFlow (the lifecycle of a flow's actors in Ray).

rayflow/events/bus.py.description: The global EventBroker Ray actor: namespaced fire-and-forget pub/sub that lets one flow's EmitEvent trigger another flow's OnEvent/OnVariableChange.

rayflow/mcp/__init__.py.description: Re-exports create_mcp, the entry point for Rayflow's MCP layer.

rayflow/mcp/server.py.description: Builds the FastMCP server exposing a curated set of tools (get_guide, list_nodes, validate_flow, create_flow, run_flow, test_flow, etc.) that reuse the editor's REST logic, so an LLM agent can build/validate/run flows autonomously.

rayflow/nodes/__init__.py.description: Re-exports the node-authoring API: decorators, pin descriptors, ExecContext, and NodeCatalog.

rayflow/nodes/builtin/__init__.py.description: Imports CallFlow so it's registered as part of the builtin module set.

rayflow/nodes/builtin/cast.py.description: Builtin explicit type-casting nodes: ToInt, ToFloat, ToStr, ToBool.

rayflow/nodes/builtin/compare.py.description: Builtin pure comparison/boolean-logic nodes (no exec pins): GreaterThan, LessThan, Equal, NotEqual, Not, And, Or, etc.

rayflow/nodes/builtin/control.py.description: Builtin control-flow nodes: OnStart, FlowOutput, Branch, Sequence, Parallel (fork/join), ForEach, While, and Map.

rayflow/nodes/builtin/events.py.description: Builtin event nodes: OnEvent (entry point triggered by the event bus), OnVariableChange (entry point triggered by a watched variable), and EmitEvent.

rayflow/nodes/builtin/flow.py.description: The CallFlow builtin node: declares the pins for running a subflow, orchestrated directly by the engine rather than via its own run().

rayflow/nodes/builtin/variables.py.description: Builtin state nodes: Get (reads a GraphState variable) and Set (writes one).

rayflow/nodes/decorators.py.description: Defines the node-authoring contract: the Input/Output/ExecInput/ExecOutput pin descriptors, the @ray_node/@engine_node/@parallel_node decorators, NodeMeta/PinSpec metadata, and the unified ExecContext used by every node's run().

rayflow/nodes/loader.py.description: NodeCatalog: discovers and registers node classes from a directory or from the workspace's custom_nodes/ package, handling pickling concerns for Ray worker distribution.

rayflow/nodes/registry.py.description: Builds and caches the process-wide singleton NodeCatalog from the builtin node modules plus the workspace's custom nodes; reset_catalog() forces a rebuild for tests/hot-reload.

rayflow/schema/__init__.py.description: Re-exports the schema dataclasses (PinKind, PinDef, NodeDef, FlowDef, VariableDef) and load_flow.

rayflow/schema/loader.py.description: Parses a flow JSON (file, path, or dict) into a FlowDef, and detects unknown/typo'd schema keys to surface as validation warnings.

rayflow/schema/models.py.description: The flow JSON schema's dataclasses: NodeDef, FlowDef, VariableDef, PinDef/PinKind, including the internal fields flatten() uses to track CallFlow splicing.

rayflow/server.py.description: FastAPI app factory for `rayflow serve`: builds the REST API for served flows, mounts the editor router, the custom-nodes router, the MCP server at /mcp, and the static editor frontend.

rayflow/state/actor.py.description: The GraphState Ray actor: a loaded flow's persistent named-variable memory across runs, plus variable-watch registration that publishes change events.

rayflow/state/queue.py.description: The RunQueue Ray actor: a per-flow FIFO event queue (one sub-queue per run_id) that the engine pushes to and FastAPI drains via blocking get() to stream SSE.

rayflow/types.py.description: The data-pin type system: closed registry of primitives (int/float/str/bool/list/dict/Any), generics (list[T], dict[str,V]), and strict compatibility checking with no implicit coercion.

rayflow/workspace.py.description: Working-directory conventions: locates/creates custom_nodes/ and flows/, builds the Ray runtime_env that distributes custom_nodes/ to workers, and resolves a flow name or path.

rayflow_file_map.json.description: Generated flat map of every tracked repo file to a short English description plus depends_on/dependents edges (computed from real imports). Not shipped in the PyPI package. Consumed by the .claude/hooks/*.py scripts to give Claude Code per-file context automatically.

signatures/cla.json.description: Generated file (written by the CLA Assistant GitHub Action) recording which external contributors have signed the CLA.

tests/__init__.py.description: Empty package marker for the test suite.

tests/test_build.py.description: Tests for the build/validation layer: BuildError cases for unknown node types, missing entry nodes, missing exec inputs, type mismatches, and data cycles.

tests/test_callflow.py.description: Tests for the CallFlow node: isolated vs. shared subflow state, meta['flow'] attribution, CallFlow inside a Parallel branch, and nested CallFlows.

tests/test_custom_nodes.py.description: Tests for convention-based custom node discovery (custom_nodes/ + flows/ in the cwd) and the runtime_env it produces for Ray workers.

tests/test_editor.py.description: Tests for the /editor/* FastAPI routes: node catalog, types, validation (including multi-error collection and warnings), flow CRUD, execution, custom nodes, concurrency isolation, events, guide/examples, and the self-verification /test endpoint.

tests/test_events.py.description: Tests for the EventBroker: cross-flow EmitEvent/OnEvent pub/sub, OnVariableChange triggering on variable writes, and namespace isolation.

tests/test_executor.py.description: End-to-end execution tests with Ray: fan-out, Parallel fork/join (including nested), AND/OR exec_in join semantics, While/Map nodes, pure comparisons, and per-run output isolation (the RunContext regression test).

tests/test_mcp.py.description: Tests for the curated MCP tool layer (rayflow/mcp/server.py), exercised through FastMCP's in-memory client.

tests/test_nodes.py.description: Tests for the node definition and discovery system: decorators, pin descriptors, and NodeCatalog registration.

tests/test_schema.py.description: Tests for flow JSON schema deserialization (load_flow / FlowDef parsing).

tests/test_server.py.description: Tests for the REST API used by `rayflow serve` (rayflow/server.py): served-flow listing, running, and duplicate-name validation.

.claude/hooks/stale_reminder.py.description: Stop hook: scans the session transcript for Edit/Write calls made since the last check; if any touched a mapped file, blocks once (decision:block, guarded by stop_hook_active to avoid looping) to remind the model to verify/update that file's description in rayflow_file_map.json before ending the turn.
